reason: Setting a GRUB2 boot loader password modifies bootloader configuration files and requires regeneration of the GRUB configuration. An incorrect change can prevent the system from booting or lock out console access, which aligns with the policy's classification of bootloader changes as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Potential boot failure if GRUB configuration is malformed; Loss of console access if password is forgotten or misapplied; Impact on system availability requiring physical rescue media
